title: IT Projektmanager (m/w/d)
required_skills: Project management methodology competence (agile, classic or hybrid)
Problem-solving and conflict resolution under pressure
Leadership and communication skills to engage and motivate teams
Analytical and structured quality-conscious working style
bonus_skills: Experience in tech and supply chain services environment
Agile delivery experience with lived agility
matched_skills: Project management methods — agile/Scrum via Hapag-Lloyd sprint ceremonies, programme governance via HSBC
Cross-functional team coordination as central interface (HSBC: 6 workstreams across product, engineering, marketing)
Budget and resource tracking (HSBC: USD 8M programme with burn-rate dashboards)
Risk matrix and escalation management (HSBC: risk escalation and prioritisation decisions)
Stakeholder communication and reporting at executive level (HSBC C-suite, Hapag-Lloyd senior stakeholders)
English C1; German B1 (partial match — C1/C2 required)
missing_skills: IT/software project leadership background (end-to-end IT project ownership)
Tech and supply chain services industry knowledge
core_responsibilities: Full project steering from A to Z: resource planning, tracking progress, budgets and timelines (controlling)
Proactive risk management: identify risks early, create risk matrices and initiate countermeasures
Quality assurance: ensure project results meet defined requirements and quality standards
Team leadership and motivation through persuasion, empathy and clear vision
Central communication interface: clear expectation setting, active listening, translating complex topics
recommended_emphasis: HSBC: Central operational interface across 6 workstreams with risk escalation and budget dashboards — directly maps to controlling, risk management and stakeholder interface responsibilities
Hapag-Lloyd: Agile sprint coordination — evidences lived agility the role description explicitly values
JIRA and Confluence tools — expected PM toolset for IT project management
ECE Group: Project spreadsheet optimisation and progress tracking — reinforces analytical, quality-conscious approach
summary: Boru scores 60 (moderate match) with well-matched programme coordination, risk management, budget tracking and agile delivery experience. The role is broadly defined and focuses on PM competencies rather than deep technical IT expertise, making Boru's transferable background viable. The key gap is German language level — the role requires C1/C2 and Boru holds B1, which may be a barrier for daily client communication in a Gütersloh-based role. Highlight programme governance depth and agile exposure; address German language level proactively in the application.
